title: Comparatives & Superlatives
description: Stupňování přídavných jmen a příslovcí — pravidla a výjimky.

vocabulary.en: expensive → more expensive → the most expensive
interesting → more interesting → the most interesting
beautiful → more beautiful → the most beautiful
difficult → more difficult → the most difficult

notes: Short vowel + consonant → double: big → bigger → biggest
Ends in -y → ier/iest: happy → happier → happiest
✓ Comparative + THAN: Prague is bigger THAN Brno.
✓ AS...AS: She is as tall as her brother.
✓ TOO + adj: It's too expensive. (more than I want)
✓ Adj + ENOUGH: She is old enough to drive.

examples.en: The Nile is the longest river in the world.
examples.cz: Nil je nejdelší řeka na světě.

examples.en: Electric cars are more expensive than petrol cars.
examples.cz: Elektrická auta jsou dražší než benzínová.

examples.en: This is the most interesting book I've ever read.
examples.cz: Toto je nejzajímavější kniha, kterou jsem kdy četl/a.

examples.en: She speaks English more fluently than her brother.
examples.cz: Mluví anglicky plynněji než její bratr.

examples.en: Is the train faster than the bus?

quiz.sentence: This is the ___ (bad) film I've ever seen.
She is ___ (good) at maths than her sister.
The exam was ___ (difficult) than I expected.
He is as ___ (tall) as his father.

tasks: 1. Compare three cities you know using comparatives and superlatives
2. Write 5 sentences using 'as...as' and 5 using 'not as...as'
3. Write a product review comparing two items you own
